content_blocks.text:
538 
chapter 16
It won’t compile!
Something’5 wrong... the Collections class clearly shows there’5 a 
sort() method, that takes a List. 
ArrayList is-a List, because ArrayList implements the List interface, 
50... it should work. 
But it doesn’t!
The compiler says it can’t find a sort method that takes an 
ArrayList<Song>, 50 maybe it doesn’t like an ArrayList of Song 
objects? It didn’t mind an ArrayList<String>, 50 what’5 the 
important difference between Song and String? What’5 the 
difference that’5 making the compiler fail?
File Edit Window Help Bummer
%javac Jukebox3.java
Jukebox3.java:15: cannot find symbol
symbol : method sort(java.util.ArrayList<Song>)
location: class java.util.Collections
 Collections.sort(songList);
 ^
1 error
And of course you probably already asked yourself, “What would it 
be sorting on?” How would the sort method even know what made 
one Song greater or less than another Song? Obviously if you want 
the song’5 title to be the value that determines how the songs are 
sorted, you’11 need some way to tell the sort method that it needs 
to use the title and not, say, the beats per minute.
We’11 get into all that a few pages from now, but first, let’5 find out 
why the compiler won’t even let us pass a Song ArrayList to the 
sort() method.
Collections.sort()

538 
chapter 16
It won’t compile!
Something’5 wrong... the Collections class clearly shows there’5 a 
sort() method, that takes a List. 
ArrayList is-a List, because ArrayList implements the List interface, 
50... it should work. 
But it doesn’t!
The compiler says it can’t find a sort method that takes an 
ArrayList<Song>, 50 maybe it doesn’t like an ArrayList of Song 
objects? It didn’t mind an ArrayList<String>, 50 what’5 the 
important difference between Song and String? What’5 the 
difference that’5 making the compiler fail?
